knimeNode.name: Spark Unpivot(Hyim)
knimeNode.shortDescription: Transforms a Spark DataFrame/RDD from wide format to long format by converting selected value columns into variable/value rows.

intro.p: This node performs an unpivot (melt) operation on a Spark DataFrame/RDD. It transforms wide-format data into long-format data by converting selected value columns into two new columns: a variable column (containing the original column names) and a value column (containing the original cell values).
Retained columns (e.g. ID columns) are kept as-is and repeated for each value column that is unpivoted.
For example, a table with columns [ID, Q1, Q2, Q3] where Q1-Q3 are value columns would be transformed into [ID, variable, value] with one row per original value column.
The node uses Spark's Dataset.unpivot() API (Spark 3.4+).

tab.name: Retained Columns
tab.option: Select the columns that should be retained (kept as-is) in the output. These are typically ID or key columns. Retained columns will be repeated for each unpivoted value column row. Column data types are displayed next to each column name for convenience.

tab.name: Value Columns
tab.option: Select the columns that should be unpivoted (melted) into rows. Each selected column will generate one row per input row in the output, with the column name stored in the variable column and the cell value stored in the value column. Column data types are displayed next to each column name for convenience.

tab.name: Options
tab.option: The name of the output column that will contain the original column names. Default: "variable".
The name of the output column that will contain the original cell values. Default: "value".
If checked, rows where the value is missing (null) will be omitted from the output.

option.name: Cast all value columns to String

tab.option: If checked, all value columns will be cast to String type before unpivoting. This is useful when value columns have mixed types (e.g. Integer, Double, and String), which would otherwise cause a type mismatch error.

Controls the sort order of the output rows. "No sorting" keeps the default Spark ordering. "Sort by retained columns" orders by the retained (ID) columns. "Sort by variable column" orders alphabetically by the variable column.

Displays the estimated number of output rows based on the input row count and the number of selected value columns (input rows x value columns). The input row count is automatically fetched from Spark when the dialog opens. When "Skip missing values" is enabled, the actual output may be less than the estimate.

An optional table that allows customizing the values in the variable column. By default, the variable column contains the original column names. Edit the "Variable Label" column to rename specific values (e.g. change "salary_2023" to "Salary").

tab.name: Validation
tab.option: Validates the unpivot configuration by executing a test unpivot job against the Spark context. Displays a sample of up to 5 output rows on success. The upstream node must be executed for this validation to work.

ports.outPort: Spark DataFrame/RDD in long format with variable and value columns.
